paragraphs.en: Also: France in Westchester at Caramoor, a taut “Henry VI,” Djo’s pop-rock spark, and more.
paragraphs.zh: 另请关注：韦斯特彻斯特的卡拉莫尔（Caramoor）法国周、紧绷而精悍的《Henry VI》、Djo 的流行摇滚火花，等等。

paragraphs.en: The choreographer Mark Morris is famously musical—“I like music more than I like anything else,” he has said—and his musical tastes are famously eclectic. He once made a dance—“O Rangasayee”—to South-Indian Carnatic music, but he has also used Schubert, Vivaldi, and the Violent Femmes. Since the beginning, though, Morris, who grew up in Seattle in the nineteen-sixties, has had a soft spot for Americana, in all its forms. “American music happens to be vast, contradictory, and full of invention, so there’s plenty to work with,” he said recently, of this vein of his career.
paragraphs.zh: 编舞家马克·莫里斯以“我最喜欢的不是别的，而是音乐”而闻名于世，同时他的音乐口味也向来包罗万象。早年他曾以南印第安的卡纳提克（Carnatic）音乐创作了舞蹈《O Rangasayee》，但他也曾使用过舒伯特、维瓦尔第，以及《暴躁的激烈女性》（Violent Femmes）的音乐。尽管如此，自上世纪六十年代在西雅图长大以来，莫里斯一直对各种形式的“美国音乐”情有独钟。“美国音乐恰恰广阔、矛盾，而且充满创造力，因此有足够的素材可供创作，”他最近在谈到这条职业脉络时说道。

paragraphs.en: A two-week engagement for his company, Mark Morris Dance Group, at the Joyce (July 14-25), reflects this variety. One program leans toward the recognizable, with offerings ranging from Gershwin (“Three Preludes,” a solo he originally created for himself, in 1992) to James P. Johnson, the pianist who invented the jazz style known as stride piano. This program also includes the only new work of the run, “Pizzica,” set to “Grande Tarantelle,” a jaunty piece by the New Orleans pianist Louis Moreau Gottschalk. (If it sounds familiar, that’s because it was popularized by George Balanchine, in 1964, with his faux-Italian duet, “Tarantella.” Morris, who loves folk dances, actually throws in some tarantella steps.)
paragraphs.zh: 他率领的马尔克·莫里斯舞蹈团在乔伊斯中心为期两周的驻场（7月14日至25日）就体现了这种多样性。第一套节目更偏向熟悉的听觉体验，作品范围从格什温《Three Preludes》（这部原为他于1992年为自己创作的独舞）到詹姆斯·P·约翰逊——发明了“stride piano”（踏步钢琴）爵士风格的钢琴家。该节目也包括本次演出中的唯一新作《Pizzica》，编舞采用了新奥尔良钢琴家路易斯·莫罗·戈特沙克（Louis Moreau Gottschalk）的轻快乐曲《Grande Tarantelle》为配乐。（如果你觉得熟悉，那是因为1964年乔治·巴兰钦（George Balanchine）用这支乐曲为其伪意大利二重舞《Tarantella》编舞而走红。蒙里斯本人喜爱民间舞蹈，节目里也确实加入了一些塔兰泰拉步法。）

paragraphs.en: Country-and-Western tunes dominate the second program, which closes with the folksy but also rather melancholy “Going Away Party,” made up of songs, by Bob Wills and His Texas Playboys, with titles such as “Milk Cow Blues.” Most of the real bonbons, however, can be found in the third program, devoted to works by the West Coast modernists Henry Cowell and Lou Harrison, including Morris’s savage pas de deux “Jenn and Spencer”—imagine a breakup gone terribly wrong—and the mysterious and ritualistic “Grand Duo,” a great stomping dance that closes with a thrilling, yet not at all upbeat, polka.—Marina Harss
paragraphs.zh: 第二套节目以乡村与西部风格曲目为主，最后以质朴却带有一丝忧郁的《Going Away Party》收束，这部作品由鲍勃·威尔斯与“His Texas Playboys”组合的歌曲组成，包含《Milk Cow Blues》等曲目。然而，真正的“甜点”在第三套节目里才出现，该节目献给西海岸现代主义者亨利·考威尔（Henry Cowell）和卢·哈里森（Lou Harrison）的作品，其中包括莫里斯那段野性十足的双人舞《Jenn and Spencer》——仿佛一场彻底失控的分手——以及神秘而仪式化的《Grand Duo》，一支有力而有节奏的踩点舞，在惊心动魄却并不轻快的波尔卡中结束。——玛丽娜·哈斯

paragraphs.en: The director Stephen Brown-Fried’s taut adaptation of the infrequently performed trilogy of history plays “Henry VI”—condensed into two three-ish-hour parts—is crowded with coups, rebellions, dynastic feuds, and beheadings, here memorably signified by bundles of rope slipping from actors’ hands. This production, by the National Asian American Theatre Company, navigates the antagonisms and the alliances with precision and economy; it is led by Jon Norman Schneider’s quietly stirring Henry and Teresa Avia Lim’s wily Queen Margaret. Actors play multiple roles, which can be confusing, but red or white roses sometimes worn by the characters help keep us on track. On a spare, blood-stained set by Kimie Nishikawa, each death accumulates weight, turning royal power struggles into a sobering reckoning with the cost of ambition.—Rhoda Feng (Public Theatre; through July 19.)
paragraphs.zh: 导演斯蒂芬·布朗-弗里德对这部不常上演的历史三部曲《亨利六世》进行的紧凑改编，被压缩为两个约三小时的部分，内容里挤满了政变、叛乱、王朝纷争和斩首场面，这些在舞台上被演员手中滑落的一束束绳索所点明。该剧由国家亚裔美国人剧团（National Asian American Theatre Company）演出，处理对立与联盟时既精准又克制；由乔恩·诺曼·施奈德饰演内敛而有力量的亨利，特蕾莎·阿维亚·林（Teresa Avia Lim）饰演老谋深算的玛格丽特女王领衔。演员们多角出演，虽然会让人一度困惑，但角色有时佩戴的红玫瑰或白玫瑰能帮助观众把握方向。在由木见·西木川（Kimie Nishikawa）打造的简洁、带血迹的舞台上，每一次死亡都逐渐增重，将王权争斗转化为对野心代价的清醒审判。—Rhoda Feng（Public Theatre；演出至7月19日）

paragraphs.en: Summer in the French countryside can be idyllic: sprawling fields of sunflowers and lavender, the southern sun reflecting off the turrets of castles, a glass of crisp white wine wherever you turn. But, alas, summer in the French countryside can also be expensive. So why not just go upstate? This year, the musical hub of Caramoor is bringing France to Westchester, with an evening of music by the Baroque composer Marc-Antoine Charpentier. Up first is the chamber opera “Les Arts Florissants,” whose characters include Peace, Discord, and Jupiter; then “La Descente d’Orphée aux enfers,” based on the myth of Orpheus. Quel plaisir!—Jane Bua (Katonah, N.Y.; July 17.)
paragraphs.zh: 法兰西乡村的夏日可以很田园诗意：一望无际的向日葵与薰衣草田，南方的阳光在城堡塔楼上反射出光芒，随处可见一杯清爽白葡萄酒。但是，唉，法兰西乡村的夏天也可能很贵。那么，为什么不直接去上州呢？今年，卡拉穆尔（Caramoor）这座音乐重镇把法国带到了威斯特切斯特，带来一个以巴洛克作曲家马尔克-安托万·夏朋蒂埃（Marc-Antoine Charpentier）音乐为主的夜晚。首先登场的是室内歌剧《花之艺术》（Les Arts Florissants），角色包括和谐、纷争与朱庇特；随后是改编自俄耳甫斯神话的《俄耳甫斯下冥》（La Descente d’Orphée aux enfers）。真是太妙了！—Jane Bua（纽约州卡托纳；7月17日）

paragraphs.en: Shortly after the conclusion of the fourth season of the Netflix blockbuster “Stranger Things,” in 2022, one of the series regulars, Joe Keery, released “Decide,” his second album as Djo. His career as a musician largely existed in the huge shadow of the show, and for a while it seemed as if it would stay that way—at least until, curiously, nearly two years later, one of the songs on “Decide,” the wistful “End of Beginning,” went viral on TikTok. The single became a bona-fide hit, and it spurred a cult following for Keery as a pop-rock sparkplug, culminating in the release of a new LP, “The Crux,” in 2025. He is joined in this show by the Australian psych-rock band Pond.—Sheldon Pearce (Forest Hills Stadium; July 17.)
paragraphs.zh: 2022年，在Netflix大热剧《怪奇物语》（Stranger Things）第四季播毕不久后，该剧常驻演员乔·基里（Joe Keery）发布了他的第二张专辑《Decide》，以Djo的身份发行。他的音乐生涯长期笼罩在该剧巨大影响之下，曾经似乎注定如此——直到奇妙的是，近两年后，这张专辑中的一首《End of Beginning》在TikTok上突然走红。该单曲成了真正的热门，点燃并巩固了基里作为流行摇滚催化者的追随者群体，最终促成了2025年新LP《The Crux》的发行。此次演出中他将与澳大利亚迷幻摇滚乐队Pond同台。—Sheldon Pearce（Forest Hills Stadium；7月17日）

paragraphs.en: Based on the gently wistful 1999 film of the same name, the musical “A Walk on the Moon” tracks the sexual awakening of Pearl (Talia Suskauer), a housewife whose marriage to the TV repairman Marty (Max Chernin) is unsettled, during the sultry summer of 1969, by an encounter with the peripatetic blouse salesman Walker (Sam Gravitte). A giant moon in the backdrop swells like a balloon—an apt metaphor for a strangely under-oxygenated production that repeatedly treats epochal events such as the moon landing as screen savers for Pearl’s self-actualization. Despite its committed performances, the show, with music by AnnMarie Milazzo and a book by Pamela Gray, traces an all too familiar emotional arc, and does little to evoke the quicksand sensation of falling in love.—R.F. (Laura Pels; through Aug. 22.)
paragraphs.zh: 改编自同名、温婉而惆怅的1999年电影《月光下漫步》（A Walk on the Moon），音乐剧聚焦珍妮（Pearl，饰演者：Talia Suskauer）在1969年闷热夏天的性觉醒——她是名处于动荡中的电视维修工马提（Marty，饰演者：Max Chernin）的家庭主妇，她与周游各地的女衬衫推销员沃克（Walker，饰演者：Sam Gravitte）的相遇改变了她。幕布后方一轮巨大的月亮不断膨胀得像气球，正好对应这部略显“缺氧”的演出：它反复把登月等时代性事件当成“屏保”，用来衬托Pearl的自我实现。尽管演出有相当投入的表演，凭借安·玛丽·米拉佐（AnnMarie Milazzo）的音乐与帕梅拉·格雷（Pamela Gray）的剧本，它仍沿用了过于熟悉的情感弧线，并且几乎没有引发“坠入爱河”时那种陷进流沙的真实触感。—R.F.（Laura Pels；演出至8月22日）

paragraphs.en: Ross McElwee, a longtime personal documentarian whose family figures prominently in his films, faces tragedy in “Remake”: the death of his son, Adrian, in 2016, at the age of twenty-seven, from an overdose of fentanyl. This loving, anguished memorial movie includes copious clips of Adrian from the director’s earlier films and from home movies. Adrian, creative and energetic from childhood on—and raised in the presence of movie cameras—became a filmmaker himself, fusing a passion for extreme skiing with his cinematic sensibility. He also struggled with mental illness and substance abuse, and had begun to make a documentary about his troubles. McElwee presents generous and poignant selections from his son’s work, while also reckoning with his own life and career—and with the practice, and the ethics, of filming his family.—Richard Brody (Film Forum; opening July 10.)
paragraphs.zh: 长期以来以个人纪录片见长的罗思·麦克威（Ross McElwee）在《Remake》中面对了家庭悲剧：他在2016年失去了儿子阿德里安（Adrian），那时阿德里安27岁，死于芬太尼过量。这部充满爱意与悲痛的悼念电影使用了大量阿德里安在导演早期影片以及家庭录像中的片段。阿德里安从小就富有创造力和活力，并在镜头前后成长——他自己也成为了一名电影人，将对极限滑雪的热爱与电影感知能力结合在一起。他也曾与精神疾病和物质依赖长期斗争，并且已经开始拍摄一部关于自己困境的纪录片。麦克威在片中慷慨且动人地精选了儿子的作品，同时也反思自己的生活与职业，以及拍摄家庭这一行为本身及其伦理问题。— 理查德·布罗迪（Richard Brody，影厅影展栏目；7月10日首映。）

paragraphs.en: The teeming series “Immigrant Nation: People in Transit,” ongoing at MOMA, offers more than fifty films from around the world that document, dramatize, and analyze the fundamental phenomenon of migration. Some notable works stay close to home, such as Clara Law’s 1990 crime drama, “Farewell China,” starring Tony Leung Ka-fai as an undocumented man who travels to New York City in search of his missing wife, played by Maggie Cheung. Other international features include Jean Renoir’s doom-laden plein-air melodrama “Toni,” from 1935, filmed on location in a mining town in Provence, where laborers from Italy and Spain, along with their native French bosses, get entangled in romantic conflicts and schemes of predatory greed, unleashing operatic passion and tragic violence.—R.B. (Through Sept. 9.)
paragraphs.zh: 正在大都会博物馆（MOMA）持续展出的《Immigrant Nation: People in Transit》系列，展示了来自世界各地五十余部电影，记录、戏剧化并分析了迁徙这一根本性现象。其中一些重要作品更接地气，例如1990年的克拉拉·罗（Clara Law）犯罪片《Farewell China》，主角由梁朝伟（Tony Leung Ka-fai）饰演一位无证身份的男子，为了寻找由张曼玉（Maggie Cheung）饰演的失踪妻子而前往纽约市。其他国际代表作还包括让·雷诺阿（Jean Renoir）于1935年的厄运弥漫的“现场写实”抒情片《Toni》，该片在普罗旺斯的一座矿镇取景拍摄，片中来自意大利和西班牙的矿工与当地法国老板卷入三角恋与掠夺性贪婪的阴谋，最终爆发出歌剧式的激情与悲剧性暴力。— R.B.（展期至9月9日。）

paragraphs.en: Taran Dugal encounters spirits steps from Green-Wood Cemetery.
paragraphs.zh: 塔兰·杜加尔（Taran Dugal）在格林伍德公墓（Green-Wood Cemetery）附近与“灵魂”相遇。

paragraphs.en: The art of carousing has long featured a mystical element. In the Middle Ages, alchemists began calling distilled alcohol “aqua vitae,” the water of life, and regarded it as the soul of a drink—hence the term “spirits.” Folk, a new cocktail lounge situated just a stone’s throw from Green-Wood Cemetery, in South Slope, follows in that occult tradition. To two first-timers, the bar’s fittingly morose, all-black exterior cut an unassuming figure on the quiet block. Inside, soft house music played, patrons awash in dim red light. The menu featured an assortment of cocktails loosely inspired by the distances people travel to live in, or to leave, Brooklyn, showcasing the bar’s effort to embrace a different kind of spirit. The guests ordered the Mangalorean, named after the chef Jay Kumar’s birthplace, an Indian city on the Arabian Sea: a sublime, sweet, velvety mix of rum, pineapple, lime, and coconut cream infused with amchur, a tangy spice made from unripe mangoes. Their second drink, the Mexpipe, Puerto Escondido, was a tequila-based number with a beachy feel, honoring one of western Mexico’s most iconic stretches of surf. Next, they ventured back to South Asia with the Roti Tagliatelle, an experimental small plate subbing out pasta for hand-cut flatbread, and served with scrumptious, steaming minced lamb. Sweet teeth practically chattering, the newcomers ended their night with the bar’s eponymous sundae, a gourmet Madagascan-vanilla ice cream topped with rose-water syrup and cashews. They left a few minutes before sunset. At the end of the block, just past Green-Wood’s fenced exterior, a small army of gravestones cast long shadows in the evening. Maybe it was the bartender’s heavy pour, or the cemetery air, but the pair felt that they understood what those medieval alchemists were talking about.
paragraphs.zh: 纵饮的艺术长期以来就带着神秘色彩。在中世纪，炼金术士开始把蒸馏酒称作“生命之水（aqua vitae）”，并把它视为酒的灵魂，于是有了“spirits”这一说法。位于南坡（South Slope）、距格林伍德公墓仅一箭之遥的新鸡尾酒酒吧 Folk，延续了这种带有神秘传统的精神。对于两位初次造访的人来说，酒吧那副恰到好处的阴郁、全黑外观，在安静街区里显得不起眼。店内播放着柔和的现场音乐，顾客浸在昏暗的红光中。菜单上有一系列鸡尾酒，灵感松散地来自人们为居住在布鲁克林或离开布鲁克林而进行的各种“距离”——也借此展示酒吧想要拥抱的另一种“灵魂”。两位顾客点了“曼加洛（Mangalorean）”，这是以主厨杰·库玛（Jay Kumar）的故乡命名的名字——一座坐落于阿拉伯海畔的印度城市——这是一款崇高而甜美、丝滑的鸡尾酒，由朗姆酒、菠萝、青柠和椰浆调成，并加入了用未熟芒果制成的酸辣香料阿姆丘儿（amchur）。他们的第二杯是“墨西哥风情湾（Mexpipe, Puerto Escondido）”，一款以龙舌兰为基底、带有海滩气息的调酒，向墨西哥西海岸最具代表性的冲浪海段致敬。接着他们又回到南亚风味，品尝了“罗蒂扁意大利面（Roti Tagliatelle）”，这道试验性小食用手工切制薄饼代替意大利面，搭配香嫩热腾腾的碎羊肉。甜味几乎让他们的牙齿都在发抖，那对新客人最后以酒吧同名圣代收尾：一球马达加斯加香草高端冰淇淋，上面浇有玫瑰水糖浆和腰果。离开时已接近日落。到了街区尽头，越过格林伍德高栅栏，晚霞中一小支墓碑“军团”投下了很长的影子。也许是调酒师杯口倒酒得太重，也许是墓园里的空气——但这对人确实有种感觉：他们仿佛懂得了中世纪炼金术士当年的那番说法。

paragraphs.zh: 附注：互联网上的精彩内容：
